ask: One query box, three modes — the text survives switching. ANSWER asks the structured KB (kb_ask): fit-gated cards and graph context with a confidence band; it abstains rather than guess. PASSAGES is raw retrieval over the ingested corpus (dense + sparse + rerank) with scores — the tool for eyeballing ranking quality. LIBRARY searches the indexed document library lexically (configure it under Settings → Library) — the exact ranked path Vinkona's research loop calls, with latency shown in the banner.
raw: Random ingested chunks, straight from the store. The place to spot OCR garbage, boilerplate, or mis-sectioned documents before they poison distillation — if the raw text is bad, everything downstream is bad.
nodes: Distilled concept nodes — the 'learnings' the distiller extracted, each with meaning, facets (epistemic status, time frame, trust tier, domain) and provenance back to source chunks. This is what kb_ask reasons over.
edges: Typed relations between nodes (causes, treats, inhibits …) with mechanism and regime, plus meta-links (disagrees_with / alternative_to / context_variant_of) — the window into how reconciliation handled conflicting sources.
cards: Procedure and criteria cards — the 'how' knowledge: step sequences, recognition criteria (must / may / must-not features the fit-gate can rule out on), staging and recommendation grades. Built by distill, refined in place by 'refine'.
sources: The source registry: every ingested document with its trust tier and epistemic regime. Trust here decides how much weight a source's claims get at distillation and adjudication; the 'source' CLI verb can rename, regroup or relicense entries.
adjudication: The node-merge queue: cases where link_to_node found an ambiguous match ('is this the same concept?'). Approving merges them; rejecting keeps them separate. Run the 'adjudicate' operation to let the LM propose resolutions in bulk.
gaps: Questions the KB could not answer (kb_ask returned nothing usable). The research loop reads these to decide what to go and learn next — an empty tab means no unmet demand has been recorded.
bundles: Brains: each source group is a bundle, exportable to its own .kdb file (split) and shippable. Load/unload switches a brain on/off for answering — instant, reversible, persists. 'Import brain…' absorbs a shipped .kdb into the master at low trust (check 'keep trust' only for your own files); eject exports a bundle to its .kdb, then removes it from the master (shared rows survive). Scenarios still pick bundle sets per session; the overlay can be SQLCipher-encrypted.
library: The search-only document library: a big uncurated folder tree indexed lexically (FTS) but NOT distilled — for the research loop to search through. Pick which subfolders are indexed here; 'collection' filters searches by topic folder.
paths: Every directory and file location the host uses — ingest roots, the Wikipedia ZIM, the data stores, bundle/metrics/control locations — one row each. Values are validated on the server before anything is written: absolute paths only, folders must exist where required, and config.toml is edited in place (comments preserved). Rows marked 'restart' are read once at process start; 'live' rows apply immediately. Moving a data-store path does NOT move its data — move the file or folder yourself first (host stopped), then point the key at the new location. Library folders are managed on the Library tab, which validates subfolder containment.
ops: Run maintenance jobs from the browser: ingest new documents, distill chunks into nodes/cards, recard (cards-only re-pass over already-distilled chunks — harvests the conversational card families without re-emitting nodes or edges), link and refine the graph, adjudicate merges, and bulk-import the external commonsense/causal datasets (import-conceptnet / import-atomic / import-glucose / import-causenet). One job at a time (they contend on the GPU lease and the KB); output streams below. The Import formats table shows which file types this install can read, and External datasets shows which bulk imports are ready to run. `dedupe` is the janitor: it finds chunks holding text the corpus already has — the same research drop re-exported under a new name, one document filed twice — and marks them against the chunk that owns the text so they are never distilled again. Nothing is deleted (search still finds them either way). Distillation runs the same check inline; the sweep is for what is already stored. Add --near for the same answer written twice in different words, which reports only until you add --apply.
autopilot: The prioritizer: an ordered list of operations the host runs automatically when idle. After each step it re-checks from the top, so higher entries preempt lower ones — e.g. distilling Vinkona's research drops (distill with bundle=vinkona) before the big document backlog (a second distill step, no bundle). A step that finds no work — or fails — stands aside for one idle re-check interval so the steps below it actually run. Hover any argument field for what it does; the bundle field offers your existing brains. With respect-leases on, the whole plan pauses while the assistant is using the LMs.
settings: The host's config.toml, editable in place: embed endpoint, LM endpoints for distillation, ports, auth token, source folders. Changes save to the file; most take effect on the next job, but port/auth changes need a restart.
import: What this knowledge host can ingest. Point 'sources' (Settings) at folders of documents and run 'ingest'; formats marked ✗ need their parser installed first — the command is shown. The library (search-only) accepts the same formats via 'ingest-library'. Wikipedia arrives as a Kiwix .zim via 'ingest --wikipedia'; Vinkona's research drops are plain .md files exported into a sources folder and distil ahead of everything else via the 'vinkona' bundle.
network: The egress broker's home: every byte that leaves this box goes through amiga_net, and this tab shows and steers it. At the top sits the LEAK CHECK — a read-only scan graded with traffic lights (good / worth fixing / UNSAFE / not verified — and 'not verified' is deliberately its own grey state: a check that couldn't run is never a pass): what listens where (loopback vs LAN, token-gated or not, plus open ports that aren't vinur's), whether the egress policy is really lease-only, whether RUNNING engines carry the offline environment (read from /proc, not assumed), WireGuard overlay presence when the box is LAN-exposed, token file permissions, shell-proxy hygiene, and the honest standing note that install-time fetches are unconfined by design. Every non-green light names its one-line fix. Below it: Rules & leases — each policy rule with its purpose, an open lease's remaining time/uses and a REVOKE button (ends a transfer mid-flight; partials stay resumable), and a per-rule Disable/Enable kill switch (audited, edits egress.toml in place). Traffic — per-rule requests, bytes each way, denials and auth rejects with last activity; denials with NO matching rule are called out. Recent decisions — the audit log's tail. Settings — outbound proxy (http_proxy / https_proxy / all_proxy / no_proxy; URL form, credentials allowed and shown REDACTED — retype to change, the *** display form is refused on save; no_proxy always gains loopback and the declared serving hosts so the box never proxies its own LM calls), the transfer engine for pulls (auto picks aria2c > wget > the built-in stream; force one if a proxy or firewall dislikes segmented downloads), and the Hugging Face token for gated repos (write-only: reads back as set/not-set plus its last characters; held by the BROKER alone via egress.toml's rule auth — inference engines run offline and never see it). Below the settings sits the broker's window, same output as ./vinur.sh net: the policy in plain language (deny by default, lease-only rules that grant nothing between operations), any lease open right now, and the most recent audited egress events; the audit log path is shown — one JSON line per decision, byte counts but never bodies or tokens. Everything here applies to the NEXT pull/search: jobs read config at launch.
serving: Which models THIS box hosts (the [serving] table in config.toml) and where they stand: service state (up / standby / dead, with the last log line), weights on disk (ready / incomplete / missing — 'incomplete' covers both a download in progress and one that died; restarting the service resumes it), and the exclusive-swap control for models too big to co-reside in VRAM. Each model row has a TUNE button, split in two: MODEL TUNING — every engine-fit knob (context window, VRAM share, parallel sequences, prefill budget, KV-cache dtype, prefix caching for vLLM; context size and GPU layers for llama), each with a plain-language explanation and a one-click recommended value — saves to tune.toml BESIDE THE WEIGHTS (per GGUF file for llama), so every model keeps its own tuning and it travels through Deploy and Swap: pick a different model and its own settings load, nothing inherited from the last resident. Older configs upgrade themselves — tuning keys still on the config.toml entry keep working (they override the file) and the first Tune save migrates them into the file. SERVICE ENTRY — the slot's own properties (exclusive / boots-first for the swap group, the port, and for containers the image and runtime), saved on the [[serving.llms]] entry in config.toml. Apply semantics are stated per row: an empty field means the engine's own default (the key is removed); ordinary knobs apply on that service's next Restart or swap-in; exclusive/boots-first/port/runtime change what the supervisor froze at start and need ./vinur.sh restart. The editor's header shows the model's weight size beside the box's VRAM so VRAM-share choices are informed, and the Stats tab (waiting queue, KV-cache %) shows what a knob actually did. The model name is a PICKER when more than one eligible model is on disk: it lists every model this entry's engine can serve (complete broker-store or hub-cache snapshots for vllm/container, GGUF files under models/ for llama), and choosing one rewrites that entry's model in config.toml and restarts the service with it — get more choices with Ops › find (searches the hub with size + fits-this-machine verdicts) and pull. 'Swap in' stops the resident model, loads this one, and waits for /health — minutes for big weights. If nothing is declared, the host only answers queries and borrows LMs from the endpoints in Settings. Downloaded weights are listed with their exact on-disk path, and the footer names the Hugging Face cache root (HF_HOME, pinned by env.sh to var/cache/huggingface/hub — never ~/.cache): one models--Org--Name folder per repo, with blobs/ holding the real files, snapshots/<revision>/ a readable symlink tree into them, and any *.incomplete blobs left over from interrupted fetches counted separately so you can reclaim the space. Each row has its own controls: Start / Stop / Restart (a Stop is HELD — the watchdog will not revive it until you Start it; a Start also clears a 'gave up after 5 restarts' verdict and, for an exclusive model, routes through the swap so its sibling's VRAM is released first) and Log, which opens that service's live log tail below the table. The note column shows the line that NAMES a failure rather than the last line printed — vLLM signs off with a pydantic docs URL, so read the Log upward from there. Weights that read 'stalled' mean partial files exist but nothing has been written for minutes: the fetch is stuck (rate limit, auth, disk, network), not slow.
stats: Graphed performance history for tuning and A/B runs, banked server-side by an always-on sampler (stats_interval_s in Settings; its own var/metrics.db). Panels: GPU utilisation / VRAM / power / temperature (nvidia-smi), the vLLM batch queue (requests running vs WAITING — waiting >0 means the GPU has more work than slots) and KV-cache %, token throughput, distillation rates (chunks/concepts/relations/cards per minute) and the curation backlog. Vertical lines are events: every ops/Prioritizer job start (▶) and end (■), plus your own ⚑ marks (POST /metrics/mark) for A/B boundaries. Hover any chart for exact values; each chart has a data-table view. History survives restarts; retention is stats_keep_days.
overview: What the pipeline has produced, at a glance: counts for every layer (sources, chunks, distilled, concepts, relations, cards), the ratios that say something — relations per concept (connectivity; the link pass raises it) and cards per 100 distilled chunks (how much how-to/criteria content the corpus holds) — and the open curation queues. The layers are hierarchical: Sources are registered documents, Raw is their chunked text, Concepts/Relations/Cards are what the LM distilled from it. Browse each with the pills above; live rates are in the header and graphed under Stats.
